title: In the Castle of My Skin
author: George Lamming

setting.note: Lamming's semi-autobiographical novel follows the boy G. growing up in a Barbadian village on the edge of a plantation in the 1930s and 40s, as the old colonial order begins to crack under labour unrest and the first stirrings of independence. The island grounds it: the poor village of wooden houses, the landlord's Great House on the hill above it, the colonial village school, the town of Bridgetown where the riots come, and the sea that will finally carry the young away.

palette.note: The green of the Barbadian land and cane the villagers work but do not own, the ground whose ownership the whole book is about. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
